typography.strategy: Corporate/Enterprise 'Old Money Tech' variant. Clean, authoritative, highly legible in dense environments.
rules: NEVER use generic fonts like Inter, Roboto, or Open Sans for headings.
Always use tight letter spacing (tracking-tight) for headings above 18px.
Labels must be uppercase with wide tracking.
Use explicit text color on hover states.

colors.archetype: ORGANIC & EARTHY mixed with SWISS HIGH-CONTRAST for a LinkedIn-native feel with an elite edge.
rules: Tinted Grey Rule: Greys should subtly tint towards the LinkedIn background hue (#F3F2EF) rather than pure monochrome.
No full-screen gradients. Keep surfaces flat or use subtle crisp shadows.
Strict contrast requirements: minimum 4.5:1 for normal text.
AI moments should use ai_accent very sparingly (e.g., an 'AI-generated' badge border or text).

visual_enhancers.surface_strategy: Neumorphism (The 'Tactile' Variant) - 'Clay & Crisp'. Mix soft shadows with sharp inner contrasts.
visual_enhancers.borders: Always use a sharp 1px border (border-black/5 or border-[#E0DFDC]) to ground elements. Never rely solely on shadows.

side_panel.layout: flex flex-col h-screen bg-white overflow-y-auto overflow-x-hidden
side_panel.header: sticky top-0 z-50 bg-white/90 backdrop-blur-md border-b border-black/5 px-4 py-3 flex items-center justify-between

injected_inline_button.description: Button injected into LinkedIn DOM next to posts.
injected_inline_button.classes: inline-flex items-center gap-1.5 px-3 py-1.5 text-sm font-semibold text-slate-600 hover:text-slate-900 bg-transparent hover:bg-black/5 rounded-md transition-colors border border-transparent hover:border-black/5

post_preview_card.container: bg-[#F3F2EF] p-4 rounded-md border border-black/5 shadow-sm space-y-2 mt-4
post_preview_card.author: text-sm font-semibold text-slate-900
post_preview_card.snippet: text-sm text-slate-600 line-clamp-3 italic border-l-2 border-[#0A66C2] pl-2

tone_selector.chip_default: px-3 py-1.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium border border-slate-200 bg-white text-slate-600 hover:bg-slate-50 hover:border-slate-300 transition-all cursor-pointer
tone_selector.chip_selected: px-3 py-1.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium border border-[#0A66C2] bg-[#0A66C2]/10 text-[#0A66C2] transition-all cursor-pointer shadow-sm

comment_output_area.textarea: w-full min-h-[120px] p-4 pt-8 rounded-md border border-slate-200 bg-white text-sm text-slate-800 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#0A66C2]/20 focus:border-[#0A66C2] resize-y shadow-sm transition-all
comment_output_area.badge: absolute top-2 left-2 bg-[#4338CA]/10 text-[#4338CA] px-2 py-0.5 rounded text-[10px] font-bold uppercase tracking-wider border border-[#4338CA]/20

primary_button.classes: w-full py-2.5 bg-[#0A66C2] hover:bg-[#004182] text-white rounded-full font-semibold text-sm transition-colors shadow-sm mt-6 flex justify-center items-center gap-2

grids_and_layouts.spacing_system: Dense but legible. Side panels require efficient use of space. Use p-4 for standard containers, gap-4 for vertical stacks.
grids_and_layouts.alignment: Left-align dense text and controls. Center-align primary CTAs or empty states.
grids_and_layouts.empty_state: flex flex-col items-center justify-center p-8 text-center h-64 border border-dashed border-slate-300 rounded-lg bg-slate-50 mt-4

motion.hover: Subtle transform (-translate-y-[1px]) and shadow increase for interactive cards.
motion.entrance: Staggered fade-in (opacity 0 to 1, translate-y 4px to 0) for panel load.
motion.toast: Use sonner with a bottom-center placement, sliding in from bottom.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure all text, including placeholders, meets APCA/WCAG standards against its background.
accessibility.focus: All interactive elements must have focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#0A66C2] focus:ring-offset-1 focus:outline-none.
accessibility.aria: Use aria-live='polite' for the comment generation output area.

icons.style: Stroke width 1.5, size 16px for inline, 20px for standalone actions.

media_and_icons.images: No external images required. Rely on UI aesthetics, typography, and clear iconography.

instructions_to_main_agent: Build out the frontend as a React app capable of building into a Chrome Extension.
Main UI lives in the side panel. Provide your React components using .js or .jsx extensions (NOT .tsx, as specified).
Ensure the DOM injected button has a completely insulated CSS scope or pure inline styles/Tailwind classes that don't clash with LinkedIn's global CSS.
Use Shadcn UI components for complex interactions like Sliders or Selects, but style them precisely according to the specs above.
Ensure all data-testids are in place for key elements (e.g., data-testid='generate-btn', data-testid='tone-selector').
Never use full transparent backgrounds in dropdowns/selects; ensure all overlays have a solid background (e.g., bg-white) with sharp borders.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy and Neumorphic tactile borders.
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms.
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file.
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style.
NEVER use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯 etc for icons. Use lucide-react icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, and entrance animations.
Subtle grain textures, custom selection states, and loading skeletons separate good from extraordinary.
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable.
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute in kebab-case format.
